description: Fix every CSS/class mismatch between the OpenTabs docs site and Chrome extension side panel versus the RetroUI source code. Each story addresses a specific category of violations found in the pixel-perfect audit.

userStories.title: Fix docs hover/active press effects to match RetroUI Button pattern
userStories.description: As a user, I want all interactive elements in the docs site to use the correct RetroUI hover/active pattern so that the press effect is consistent with the RetroUI design system.
acceptanceCriteria: ThemeSelect.astro hover uses box-shadow: 1px 1px 0 0 var(--border) (shadow-xs), not box-shadow: none
ThemeSelect.astro active uses box-shadow: none and transform: translateY(2px)
Search button (site-search button[data-open-modal]) hover uses box-shadow: 1px 1px 0 0 var(--border), not none
Search button active uses box-shadow: none and transform: translateY(2px) translateX(1px)
Mobile menu button (starlight-menu-button button) hover uses box-shadow: 1px 1px 0 0 var(--border), not none
Mobile menu button active uses box-shadow: none and transform: translateY(2px) translateX(1px)
Social icon links (.social-icons a) hover uses box-shadow: 1px 1px 0 0 var(--border), not none
Social icon links active uses box-shadow: none and transform: translateY(2px) translateX(1px)
Social icon links do NOT change background on hover — remove background: var(--muted) from .social-icons a:hover
userStories.notes: RetroUI Button.tsx defines the canonical press effect: shadow-md → hover:shadow → active:shadow-none with hover:translate-y-1 active:translate-y-2 active:translate-x-1. The docs site elements use a simplified version that goes straight to shadow:none on hover instead of reducing to shadow-xs first. Files to edit: docs/src/styles/global.css (lines 286-289 for search button, 326-329 for mobile menu, 331-345 for social icons) and docs/src/overrides/ThemeSelect.astro (lines 20-27). The RetroUI shadow-xs value is '1px 1px 0 0 var(--border)'. For CSS (not Tailwind classes), use the raw value. Add :active rules wherever they are missing.

userStories.title: Replace hardcoded hex colors in docs light mode Starlight overrides
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all Starlight CSS variable overrides to use RetroUI theme tokens instead of hardcoded hex colors so that theme changes propagate correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/src/styles/global.css light mode block: --sl-color-gray-5 uses var(--muted) instead of #e5e5e5
docs/src/styles/global.css light mode block: --sl-color-gray-6 uses var(--card) instead of #f0f0f0
docs/src/styles/global.css light mode block: --sl-color-gray-7 uses var(--background) instead of #f8f8f8
Dark mode :root block includes --sl-color-gray-7: var(--card) to match the light mode override pattern
No hardcoded hex values remain in any --sl-color-* variable assignment
userStories.notes: File: docs/src/styles/global.css. Lines 172-174 in the :root[data-theme='light'] block have three hardcoded hex values. Replace: --sl-color-gray-5: #e5e5e5 → var(--muted), --sl-color-gray-6: #f0f0f0 → var(--card), --sl-color-gray-7: #f8f8f8 → var(--background). Also add --sl-color-gray-7: var(--card) to the dark mode :root block (after line 147, before line 148). The dark mode block currently maps gray-6 to var(--card) but skips gray-7 entirely.

userStories.title: Replace Starlight CSS variables in Pagination.astro with RetroUI values
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Pagination component to use standard CSS values derived from RetroUI's Text component instead of Starlight-specific CSS variables.
acceptanceCriteria: Pagination.astro .link-title font-size uses a standard CSS value (1.5rem) instead of var(--sl-text-2xl)
Pagination.astro .link-title line-height uses a standard CSS value (1.25) instead of var(--sl-line-height-headings)
Pagination.astro .link-title has font-family: var(--font-head) to match RetroUI heading style
userStories.notes: File: docs/src/overrides/Pagination.astro, lines 64-65 in the <style> block. RetroUI Text.tsx h2 variant is 'text-3xl lg:text-4xl font-semibold' and h3 is 'text-2xl font-medium'. For pagination link titles, text-2xl (1.5rem) with line-height 1.25 and font-family var(--font-head) is the right match. Replace var(--sl-text-2xl) with 1.5rem and var(--sl-line-height-headings) with 1.25. Add font-family: var(--font-head).

userStories.title: Fix side panel ToolRow to use RetroUI patterns instead of generic Tailwind
userStories.description: As a user, I want tool rows in the side panel to follow RetroUI styling conventions with proper borders and hover states.
acceptanceCriteria: ToolRow.tsx uses border-b-2 instead of border-b for the bottom separator between tools
ToolRow.tsx hover state uses hover:bg-accent instead of hover:bg-muted/10 — the /10 opacity modifier is not a RetroUI pattern

The active tool left border indicator uses bg-primary background highlight instead of border-l-2 — change active state to use a subtle bg-accent/30 background or bg-primary/10 background tint matching RetroUI's data-selected patterns
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ToolRow.tsx, line 20. Current className: `border-border hover:bg-muted/10 flex items-center gap-2 border-b px-3 py-2 transition-colors last:border-b-0 ${active ? 'border-primary border-l-2' : ''}`. Changes: (1) border-b → border-b-2 (RetroUI uses border-2 universally), (2) hover:bg-muted/10 → hover:bg-accent (matches RetroUI ghost button hover pattern from Button.tsx), (3) For active state, replace 'border-primary border-l-2' with 'bg-accent/20' or similar — RetroUI uses bg-primary/50 for table row hover (Table.tsx) and data-selected:bg-primary for tabs, so bg-accent/20 is the closest contextually appropriate pattern for a subtle active indicator.

userStories.title: Replace side panel ToolRow spinner with RetroUI Loader component
userStories.description: As a user, I want the active tool indicator to use the RetroUI Loader (bouncing dots) instead of a CSS spinner circle, since RetroUI does not have a spinning circle pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: ToolRow.tsx replaces the div with border-muted border-t-primary rounded-full animate-spin with the Loader component from retro/Loader.tsx
The Loader is rendered with size='sm' to fit the tool row height
No rounded-full border-spinner pattern remains in ToolRow.tsx
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ToolRow.tsx, line 36. Current: `<div className='border-muted border-t-primary h-3 w-3 animate-spin rounded-full border-2' />`. Replace with: `<Loader size='sm' count={2} duration={0.4} delayStep={80} />`. Import Loader from './retro/Loader.js'. The Loader component uses bouncing dots with border-2 (RetroUI pattern). size='sm' gives 8x8px dots which fits the row. Using count={2} keeps it compact. The rounded-full spinner uses border-radius: 9999px which violates RetroUI's --radius: 0 principle.

userStories.title: Fix side panel PluginCard accordion trigger to use items-start like RetroUI
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the PluginCard accordion trigger to use items-start (RetroUI's Accordion pattern) instead of items-center for consistent vertical alignment.
acceptanceCriteria: PluginCard.tsx AccordionPrimitive.Trigger className uses items-start instead of items-center
The ChevronDown icon aligns to the top of the trigger row, matching RetroUI Accordion.tsx
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx, line ~117. The AccordionPrimitive.Trigger has className with 'items-center'. RetroUI Accordion.tsx AccordionHeader uses 'items-start' (see RetroUI components/retroui/Accordion.tsx line 36: 'flex flex-1 items-start justify-between px-4 py-2 font-head cursor-pointer'). Change items-center to items-start. Note that the PluginCard uses a custom trigger layout with PluginIcon and badges inside, so items-start aligns the chevron to the top of the multi-line content, which is the correct RetroUI behavior.
